The '''Bakura Imperial Prison''' was a detention center orbiting the [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] planet of [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]]. The facility seemed to have two large solar sails and rotated fairly quickly. The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Raid at Bakura|raided]] the station in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] to rescue [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]].

==Description==
The [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]] was a heavily-armed [[Space station/Legends|space station]] with numerous [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] ready to strike any intruder coming in too close.

Apart from the turbolaser defenses, endless numbers of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] patrolled the surrounding space all the time. There were also docking bays for [[Imperial Action transport|Imperial transports]] who frequently arrived with new [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]]. Aside from the docking bays for Imperial Transports, there was also at least one external holding area for various TIES at the facility, including TIE/sa Bombers.

There were some exposed girders on the top "wheel" of the space station, hinting that the station was still under construction by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] or otherwise was undergoing repair work.

==Raid at Bakura==
In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] loss at the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], some scientists were found on the [[Hoth/Legends|ice-covered planet]]. Because of their loyalty to the Rebellion, they were arrested by the Empire and sent here.

Some time after the event, Rebel [[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] made up a plan to rescue the scientists, and [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] along with [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] were sent to the Bakura system. Upon arrival, Imperial transports were departing the station with the scientists. Wedge, however, stopped them from escaping with his [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion guns]]. [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|Rebel evacuation transports]] arrived and although the Imperial starfighter defenses repeatedly attacked, and an [[Imperial escort carrier (storm commando)|Imperial vessel]] managed to destroy the third transport that was escaping with the prisoners, the [[Raid at Bakura|battle]] ultimately became a victory for the Rebels. Because Hobbie was shot down shortly after retrieving the second batch of prisoners and Wedge Antilles' X-Wing not having room for two, the latter had to steal a TIE bomber from the prison in order to have the vehicle necessary to rescue Hobbie.

==Behind the scenes==
According to the ''Rebel Strike'' audio commentary, the orbiting prison station was created from a rough sketch based on several sci-fi movies, and then rendered to be more similar to something from the ''Star Wars'' universe.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'' {{1st}}
